title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers pathlib, content discovery, roots, symlinks, and admission into a changed bounded content-discovery manifest. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded content-discovery manifest with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.
Declare roots, extensions, ignores, hidden files, drafts, ordering, maximum files, depth, and total bytes before traversal.
Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pathlib Semantics
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 7023 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a bounded content manifest with explicit rejection, root, and collision evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pathlib Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use path and purepath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and i/o semantics instead of string slicing. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pathlib Semantics, keep Pathlib Semantics connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-discovery-manifest-1i97ga
# Competency: ssg-discovery-manifest.
# Case 7515: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_discovery_manifest_1i97ga(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1i97ga = "1i97ga-ag79i1-7515"
    assert evidence_variant_1i97ga
    evidence_axes_1i97ga = (
        "reader1i97ga",
        "source7515",
        "parseag79i1",
        "route1i97ga7515",
        "artifact75151i97ga",
        "recoveryag79i17515",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1i97ga)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"
